turns.student: How have you been feeling overall lately?
turns.patient: Honestly, I've been really struggling with my symptoms - the crampy lower abdominal pain and bloody stools are getting worse, and I'm just so tired all the time from the diarrhea. It's affecting my work performance and making me feel really worried about what might be going on.

turns.student: Tell me about your day-to-day — how has your health been in general?
turns.patient: It's been a nightmare, to be honest. I've had 8-10 bowel movements per day, which is way more than usual, and they're all loose or watery with urgency, making it hard for me to even leave my apartment sometimes, let alone keep up with my demanding work schedule as a software engineer.

turns.student: You mentioned some health issues. Can you walk me through what happens?
turns.patient: The crampy lower abdominal pain is predominantly on my left side and it's been constant for weeks now. It's like a sharp, stabbing sensation that never really goes away, even after I've had a bowel movement. And then there are the bloody stools - they're always accompanied by mucus and bright red blood mixed with stool, which is just terrifying for me.

turns.student: And when these episodes occur, what does your body do?
turns.patient: It feels like I'm constantly on edge, waiting for the next episode to hit. When it does, I feel this overwhelming sense of urgency and my body just goes into overdrive - I get this intense crampy pain in my lower abdomen that's almost unbearable, and then I have to rush to the bathroom as fast as I can because I know it won't wait for me.

turns.student: Let's focus on the episodes. Walk me through one from start to finish.
turns.patient: Okay, so like yesterday morning, I woke up around 2 am with this intense crampy pain in my lower left abdomen. It was like a sharp stabbing sensation that made me jump out of bed. I tried to ignore it at first, thinking maybe I just had a stomach bug or something, but as the minutes ticked by, the pain only intensified and I could feel the urge to have a bowel movement coming on strong. I rushed to the bathroom, and when I sat down, I knew it was going to be bad - the stool was bright red with mucus, and it was just... ugh, it's hard to describe, but it was like my body was just emptying itself out completely. The pain was still there, burning in my abdomen, and I felt this overwhelming sense of relief when it finally stopped, but at the same time, I knew that I'd be back on edge again soon, waiting for the next episode to hit.

turns.student: During an episode, do you notice anything besides the main symptom?
turns.patient: Yes, during an episode, I often feel this sensation of incomplete evacuation after most bowel movements, which is really frustrating and adds to my overall anxiety about what's going on with my body. It's like my body is just not finishing the job, you know?

turns.student: Specifically, any changes in your skin — like sweating or flushing?
turns.patient: No, I don't experience excessive sweating or flushing during these episodes. The only thing I notice is that my face might feel a bit warm due to the pain and discomfort, but it's not like I'm flushed or anything.

turns.student: And your stomach — any queasiness or nausea during these moments?
turns.patient: Actually, yes - sometimes after an episode, especially if the stool is particularly bloody or loose, my stomach can feel a bit queasy and I might get a little lightheaded. It's not always severe, but it's definitely there, which adds to my anxiety and worry about what's going on with my body.

turns.student: What about other parts of your body? Any pain, tingling, or unusual sensations?
turns.patient: To be honest, besides the crampy lower abdominal pain and the ache in both knees and ankles, I haven't experienced any other significant pain or discomfort. The joint pain is just a dull ache that's always there, but it doesn't really interfere with my daily activities too much.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats?
turns.patient: Yes, I've had low-grade fever a few times per week, which is really concerning for me. It's not like I'm running a high temperature or anything, but it's just this constant nagging feeling that something's off with my body.

turns.student: Now let's zoom out. Any swelling you've noticed anywhere on your body?
turns.patient: No, there hasn't been any noticeable swelling on my body - not even in my abdomen or around my joints where I'd expect it given the pain and discomfort I'm experiencing. Everything looks normal to me visually, but of course, that doesn't mean anything to me right now because I just know something's going on inside that I can't see.

turns.student: Have you noticed any changes in your weight or appetite?
turns.patient: Yeah, unfortunately, yes. I've unintentionally lost about 4 kg (9 lbs) over the past three weeks, and my appetite has decreased significantly due to the constant discomfort and pain from the diarrhea. It's hard for me to even think about eating when I know it might just trigger another episode.

turns.student: Let me ask about your medical history. What conditions have you been treated for?
turns.patient: I've had an appendectomy at age 16, and that was the only significant surgery I've ever had. I don't recall any complications or issues afterwards, just a pretty standard recovery process.

turns.student: Any medications you're taking? Are you taking them consistently?
turns.patient: Yes, I've been self-medicating with loperamide (Imodium) for diarrhea over the past 2 weeks, but to be honest, it's only provided minimal relief. And when I do experience joint pain, I take ibuprofen 2-3 times a week, which seems to worsen my abdominal symptoms, so I'm not sure if that's helping or hurting me in the long run.

turns.student: Tell me about your lifestyle. How active are you?
turns.patient: Honestly, I've been pretty sedentary lately due to the constant discomfort and pain from the diarrhea. I used to be an avid hiker and enjoyed outdoor activities, but since these symptoms started, I haven't had the energy or motivation to do much of anything except work and rest. My job as a software engineer is demanding, and I often find myself working 60+ hour weeks, which doesn't help with my stress levels or overall well-being.

turns.student: And your diet — what does a typical day of eating look like?
turns.patient: To be honest, my diet has been pretty irregular lately due to my long work hours and lack of energy. I mostly end up eating takeout or leftovers because it's just easier to grab something quick and go back to work. I'm not really sure what I eat anymore - sometimes I'll have a decent meal in the morning, but then I might skip lunch altogether and just snack on whatever is easy to grab at my desk. It's not healthy, I know, but when you're feeling as crummy as I do all the time, even thinking about cooking or preparing meals feels overwhelming.

turns.student: How about alcohol or smoking?
turns.patient: I've actually increased my beer consumption over the past month to 4-5 beers per night due to stress from symptoms and work. I'm a non-smoker and don't use recreational drugs, but drinking has become a way for me to cope with the anxiety and discomfort from my symptoms.

turns.student: Now family history. Any medical problems run in your family?
turns.patient: My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 30, which is definitely on my mind right now given my own symptoms. I'm worried that maybe I could be heading down a similar path.

turns.student: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious or chronic conditions?
turns.patient: My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 30, which is definitely on my mind right now given my own symptoms. She's been dealing with it for years, and I'm worried that maybe I could be heading down a similar path.

turns.student: We've covered a lot. Is there anything we haven't discussed that you think is relevant?
turns.patient: Actually, yes. I don't know if this is relevant or not, but I've noticed that my symptoms seem to have started after I traveled to Mexico for a work conference three months ago. At first, I thought it might be related to something I ate while I was there, but as the symptoms persisted, I'm starting to wonder if it could be something more serious.
